question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 lowers MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R2↔R3 upper core) from 1514
to 1400. R3 stays at default. IS-IS CSNP/PSNP exchange fails for
larger LSPs; adjacency stalls in INIT. SPF re-routes through R2's
alternate core path (R2-R6 to R6-R7 to R7-R4 to R4-R3 — only if such a
path exists; in this lab R6-R7 + R4-R7 covers it). Mass label-stack
recompute on every router.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), the MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2<->R3 upper core) was lowered to 1400 while R3 stays at 1514, creating an asymmetric L3 MTU on the point-to-point link.
Small Hello PDUs still fit, but the oversize IS-IS CSNP/PSNP/LSP exchange exceeds R2's reduced MTU and is dropped, so the link-state databases cannot synchronize and the adjacency cannot reach full operation over that link; SPF therefore reroutes traffic between the PEs over the lower-core detour, forcing a mass SR label-stack recompute on every router.
Fix: remove the MTU override on R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (restore 1514).
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; small IS-IS
hello PDUs may still cross the asymmetric MTU and keep the adjacency
Up, while large LSP/CSNP exchange fails. Diagnostic ground truth
lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: MTU 1400 (baseline: 1514). R3's Gi0/0/0/0: default — asymmetric L3 MTU on a point-to-point link.
R2 `show running-config interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: `mtu 1400` present (baseline: line absent).
Any router `ping <R3-loopback> source Loopback0 size 1500`: fragmentation-needed / drops, while `size 1000` succeeds — asymmetric MTU breaks large-packet traversal.
R2 `show isis interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: per-interface state may flag MTU mismatch; full-size LSP exchange from R3 is dropped on R2.
R0 `show route 192.0.2.3` (R3's Loopback0): if the IS-IS LSP exchange is impaired, the path may shift to a longer detour via R6/R7/R4 — diff `show route` against baseline.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no mtu
